topic: Phase 8B — bypass run_all_agents() in Apex 3.0 mode (legacy-sell leak fix)
summary: Diagnosed and fixed a migration leak introduced during the Phase 8 cutover: run_all_agents() was still firing unconditionally every scan cycle even in 3.0 mode, paying for a full Sonnet call (4096 tokens, ~1-3s latency) whose BUY output was discarded and whose SELL output was deterministic regime-change logic that never needed the LLM. Added a pre-check (_scan_cutover_pre) before the run_all_agents() call that, when USE_LEGACY_PIPELINE=False, builds the deterministic sell list directly from positions_to_reconsider and sets a minimal decision dict — skipping the Sonnet call entirely. Legacy mode (USE_LEGACY_PIPELINE=True) is fully preserved in the else branch. Rollback is unchanged: flipping USE_LEGACY_PIPELINE=True restores run_all_agents() with zero code changes.
motivation: Architecture audit revealed run_all_agents() was an accidental migration bridge, not intentional design. The Phase 8 cutover comment at bot_trading.py:2235 focused only on the buy path. No comment explained why run_all_agents() should persist in Apex mode. The Trading Analyst Sonnet call produces BUY recommendations only — in 3.0 mode those are discarded by Apex Track A. The SELL list comes entirely from check_thesis_validity() which is pure deterministic regime-change logic. Eliminating the redundant Sonnet call removes 1 of 4 Sonnet calls per scan cycle.

bot_trading.py.description: Replaced unconditional run_all_agents() call at line 2104 with an if/else gated on _scan_cutover_pre (= not should_use_legacy_pipeline()). Apex branch: builds _apex_mode_sells from positions_to_reconsider directly, sets minimal decision dict with sells populated. Legacy branch: calls run_all_agents() exactly as before. Downstream code (dashboard setup, sell loop, divergence block) is unchanged and handles both decision shapes via dict.get() with defaults.
bot_trading.py.rollback: Set USE_LEGACY_PIPELINE=True — else branch executes, run_all_agents() called as before

tests/test_apex_phase8b_agent_bypass.py.description: 10 new structural tests: phase8b block exists, cutover_pre flag reads correct function, run_all_agents inside else branch, apex branch builds sells from positions_to_reconsider, HOLD filter present, minimal decision has all required keys, legacy branch preserves run_all_agents with all args, dashboard fields populated after branch, divergence block reads decision.buys, sell loop reads decision.sells.

calls: run_all_agents() Trading Analyst — 4096 tokens — BUY output discarded, SELL output deterministic
Apex Track B apex_call — 4096 tokens — live TRIM/EXIT
Apex Track A apex_call — 4096 tokens — live new entries
Apex shadow apex_call — 4096 tokens — log only
Apex Track B apex_call — 4096 tokens — live TRIM/EXIT
Apex Track A apex_call — 4096 tokens — live new entries
Apex shadow apex_call — 4096 tokens — log only
after.eliminated: run_all_agents() Trading Analyst — wasted Sonnet call, ~1-3s latency per scan

exit_authority_3_0.forced_exits: guardrails deterministic — EOD flat, scalp timeout, architecture violations

tests.regression: 1466 passed, 1 skipped, 1 pre-existing failure (test_portfolio_optimizer::test_update_builds_valid_correlation_matrix — pre-dates this session, unrelated to scan-cycle control flow)

what_is_next: Shadow log (USE_APEX_V3_SHADOW=True) still generates 1 additional Sonnet call per cycle for divergence instrumentation. With legacy fully bypassed, the divergence comparison at line 2781 now produces an empty legacy side. Shadow can be disabled (USE_APEX_V3_SHADOW=False) once Amit is satisfied that Apex 3.0 is stable — reduces to 2 live Sonnet calls per cycle.
